subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary of the Interior (in this subchapter referred to as the “Secretary”) may carry out in accordance with this subchapter a program to be known as the “Junior Duck Stamp Conservation and Design Program” (in this subchapter referred to as the “Program”) to accomplish the goals of—

paragraph.content: providing to school children environmental education opportunities relating to the conservation and management of migratory birds; and
increasing the capacity for schools, States, and other educational programs to conduct conservation and education programs.
conducting in all interested States the activities which on the day before October 6, 1994, are conducted under the program known as the Junior Duck Stamp Conservation and Design Program;
other activities authorized under the Program by this subchapter or any other Act; and
any other activity necessary to carry out the conservation and education goals of the Program.

subsection.heading: Effort to conduct Program in all States

content.p: The Secretary shall take appropriate steps to seek to conduct the Program in all of the States.
The Secretary shall annually submit a report to the Congress on the status of the Program in each of the States.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2000—Subsec. (c). Pub. L. 106–316 struck out “50” before “States” in pars. (1) and (2).

note.heading: Short Title of 2006 Amendment
note.p: Pub. L. 109–166, § 1, Jan. 10, 2006, 119 Stat. 3576, provided that: “This Act [amending sections 719a and 719c of this title and provisions listed in a table of National Wildlife Refuges set out under section 668dd of this title] may be cited as the ‘Junior Duck Stamp Reauthorization Amendments Act of 2005’.”

note.heading: Short Title
note.p: Pub. L. 103–340, § 1, Oct. 6, 1994, 108 Stat. 3119, provided that: “This Act [enacting this subchapter and provisions listed in a table of National Wildlife Refuges set out under section 668dd of this title] may be cited as the ‘Junior Duck Stamp Conservation and Design Program Act of 1994’.”
